prompt: Japanese Edo-period ukiyo-e woodblock print in the style of Katsushika Hokusai, Thirty-Six Views of Mount Fuji series. A steep mountain dominates the composition on a strong diagonal axis. A lone traveller in traditional sedge hat and robes with a walking staff descends the mountain path, depicted at three successive positions along the slope — each lower than the last, always following the steepest descent. Dense mist rendered in bokashi horizontal gradation obscures the upper peak and the full valley below. A still lake sits at the base of the mountain — the destination. Flat unmodulated areas of Prussian blue, ochre, and grey. Bold black outlines defining every form. No cast shadows. Visible woodgrain texture in color fields. No photographic shading. Authentic Edo-period print aesthetic, aged paper texture, slight ink registration variation at edges. Negative: no Western perspective, no photographic shading, no gradients within color zones, no cast shadows, no text, no labels, no modern elements, no diagrams, no equations
tradition_approach: Hokusai diagonal mountain composition, flat unmodulated Prussian blue and ochre color zones, bold black outlines, bokashi mist gradation, no cast shadows, visible woodgrain texture in color fields.
